dashboard_recap.headline: MLB Daily Recap - April 29, 2026
dashboard_recap.article:
**Dominant Performances Highlight MLB Action**

Gavin Williams tossed 7.2 scoreless innings, striking out nine, to lead the Cleveland Guardians to a 1-3 win over the Tampa Bay Rays. Williams' impressive outing was matched by Tyler Glasnow, who fanned nine over 5.2 frames, but took the loss for the Miami Marlins. Meanwhile, Cade Cavalli struck out 10 over six strong innings to guide the Washington Nationals to a 14-2 thrashing of the New York Mets.

In other games, Hunter Goodman homered twice and drove in three runs to pace the Colorado Rockies to a 13-2 rout of the Cincinnati Reds. Alec Burleson went deep and drove in two to help the St. Louis Cardinals edge the Pittsburgh Pirates 5-4. Matt Olson homered and drove in two to lead the Atlanta Braves past the Detroit Tigers 4-3.

Nathan Eovaldi pitched seven scoreless innings, fanning seven, to secure a 0-3 win for the Texas Rangers over the New York Yankees. Luis Severino struck out eight over seven strong innings to lead the Athletics to a 5-2 victory over the Kansas City Royals.

all_games.summary: The Cleveland Guardians edged the Tampa Bay Rays 3-1 behind Gavin Williams' 7.2 shutout innings with 9 strikeouts.

all_games.game: Los Angeles Angels @ Chicago White Sox

all_games.summary: The Chicago White Sox edged the Los Angeles Angels 3-2 behind a late-game single by Colson Montgomery and a strong 7-inning outing from Erick Fedde.

all_games.summary: The Texas Rangers shut out the New York Yankees 3-0 behind Nathan Eovaldi's 7-inning performance with 7 strikeouts and Josh Jung's 2-RBI effort.

all_games.game: Boston Red Sox @ Toronto Blue Jays

all_games.summary: The Athletics defeated the Kansas City Royals 5-2 behind Luis Severino's 7-inning, 8-K performance, while Lawrence Butler's 3-RBI, 1-HR effort went for naught.
The Philadelphia Phillies edged the San Francisco Giants 0-0, with a scoreless tie ultimately deciding the outcome.

all_games.top_pitching_line: Lance McCullers Jr.: 5.0 IP, 7 K, 3 ER, 4 BB, 2 H
all_games.top_batting_line: Cam Smith: 1 H, 1 HR, 3 RBI, 3 AB
all_games.summary: The Houston Astros dominated the Baltimore Orioles 11-3, led by Cam Smith's 3 RBI and Lance McCullers Jr.'s 7 strikeouts over 5 innings.
